# Issue #8776: Fix compute_shape_offset non-tuple indexing for PyTorch >=2.9
**Repository:** Project-MONAI/MONAI
**Author:** Harrisaint
**Status:** closed
**Created At:** 2026-03-17T08:08:19Z

## Description
Fixes #8775  .

### Description

This PR resolves issue #8775 by casting spatial_shape to a tuple inside compute_shape_offset to prevent breaking changes in PyTorch >= 2.9.

### Types of changes
<!--- Put an `x` in all the boxes that apply, and remove the not applicable items -->
- [x] Non-breaking change (fix or new feature that would not break existing functionality).
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or new feature that would cause existing functionality to change).
- [ ] New tests added to cover the changes.
- [ ] Integration tests passed locally by running `./runtests.sh -f -u --net --coverage`.
- [ ] Quick tests passed locally by running `./runtests.sh --quick --unittests  --disttests`.
- [ ] In-line docstrings updated.
- [ ] Documentation updated, tested `make html` command in the `docs/` folder.

<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

Convert `spatial_shape` to a tuple before passing it to `np.array(...)` in `monai/data/utils.py` to avoid PyTorch ≥2.9 non-tuple sequence indexing issues. Add a new unit test `tests/data/utils/test_compute_shape_offset.py` that calls `compute_shape_offset` with a `torch.Size([10, 10, 10])` input and asserts the returned shape has length 3.

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 2 (Simple) | ⏱️ ~10 minutes

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

### Comment by ericspod at 2026-04-09T19:58:22Z
We have to merge #8814 before this can be merged however.
